name: PipeRider
pricing_notes: OSS CLI free; PipeRider Cloud: free tier; Pro $49/mo; Enterprise custom

auth.notes: OSS: no auth; Cloud: API token from PipeRider dashboard

performance.notes: Profiling speed depends on database query performance; HTML reports generated locally

agent_readiness.notes: Data quality profiling and dbt PR impact analysis. Runs data profiling (distributions, nulls, uniqueness) and compares before/after schema/data changes. Integrates with GitHub PR comments to show data impact of dbt changes. Supports dbt, SQLite, PostgreSQL, BigQuery, Snowflake. Python CLI.

gotchas: Primarily a dbt companion tool — less useful without dbt workflows
HTML reports are human-readable but not programmatically accessible via API
PipeRider Cloud is young — OSS CLI is more mature
best_when: dbt teams wanting PR-level data impact analysis (before/after comparison)
Data engineers wanting automated data profiling in CI/CD pipelines
Teams adopting DataOps practices with dbt + GitHub workflow
Lightweight data quality profiling without complex rule configuration
avoid_when: Non-dbt teams (limited value without dbt integration)
Production runtime data monitoring (use Evidently or Monte Carlo)
Complex assertion-based testing (Great Expectations or Soda better)
summary: PipeRider is a data quality profiling tool focused on dbt PR impact analysis. Profiles data distributions, nulls, and uniqueness, then compares before/after changes to surface data impact in GitHub PR comments. CLI tool supporting dbt, PostgreSQL, BigQuery, Snowflake. Best for dbt teams wanting automated data impact assessment in CI/CD workflows. Less mature than Soda/Great Expectations for complex rule-based testing.
